message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  SARS-CoV-2 is the coronavirus pathogen of the currently prevailing COVID-19 pandemic. It relies on its main protease (M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  ) for replication and pathogenesis. M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  is a demonstrated target for the development of antivirals for SARS-CoV-2. Past studies have systematically explored tripeptidyl inhibitors such as nirmatrelvir as M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  inhibitors. However, dipeptidyl inhibitors especially those with a spiro residue at their P2 position have not been systematically investigated. In this work, we synthesized about 30 reversibly covalent dipeptidyl M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  inhibitors and characterized them on
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  enzymatic inhibition potency, structures of their complexes with M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  , cellular M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  inhibition potency, antiviral potency, cytotoxicity, and
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  metabolic stability. Our results indicated that M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  has a flexible S2 pocket that accommodates dipeptidyl inhibitors with a large P2 residue and revealed that dipeptidyl inhibitors with a large P2 spiro residue such as (
                  <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  )-2-azaspiro[4,4]nonane-3-carboxylate and (
                  <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  )-2-azaspiro[4,5]decane-3-carboxylate have optimal characteristics. One compound MPI60 containing a P2 (
                  <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  )-2-azaspiro[4,4]nonane-3-carboxylate displayed high antiviral potency, low cellular cytotoxicity, and high
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  metabolic stability and can be potentially advanced to further preclinical tests.
                </jats:p>
title: A Systematic Survey of Reversibly Covalent Dipeptidyl Inhibitors of the SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease

reference.article-title: Learning from the Past: Possible Urgent Prevention and Treatment Options for Severe Acute Respiratory Infections Caused by 2019-nCoV
Evolutionary and Structural Insights about Potential SARS-CoV-2 Evasion of Nirmatrelvir

reference.article-title: Boceprevir, GC-376, and calpain inhibitors II, XII inhibit SARS-CoV-2 viral replication by targeting the viral main protease
Potent Noncovalent Inhibitors of the Main Protease of SARS-CoV-2 from Molecular Sculpting of the Drug Perampanel Guided by Free Energy Perturbation Calculations
MPI8 is Potent against SARS-CoV-2 by Inhibiting Dually and Selectively the SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease and the Host Cathepsin L
Evaluation of SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Inhibitors Using a Novel Cell-Based Assay
Cathepsin L plays a key role in SARS-CoV-2 infection in humans and humanized mice and is a promising target for new drug development
